findings.description: The map_harness_to_ag_ui function signature in the emitter spec and tasks omits the thread_id argument, which is required to populate the mandatory threadId field in AG-UI events.
findings.resolution: Update the map_harness_to_ag_ui signature in openspec/changes/harness-ag-ui-bridge/specs/ag-ui-emitter/spec.md and Task 4.6 in tasks.md to: async def map_harness_to_ag_ui(stream: AsyncIterator[HarnessEvent], thread_id: str) -> AsyncIterator[AGUIEvent]. Ensure the mapper scenarios reflect that the provided thread_id is used for RUN_STARTED and RUN_FINISHED events.

findings.description: ChatRequest.message lacks a maxLength constraint in the OpenAPI contract, which is a standard security gap even for local-trust v1 deployments.
findings.resolution: Add maxLength: 100000 to the message property of the ChatRequest schema in openspec/changes/harness-ag-ui-bridge/contracts/openapi/v1.yaml. This provides a sensible upper bound (approx 100k characters) for v1 inputs.

findings.description: The wp-web-cli work package is overweight, covering both the FastAPI application and the CLI subcommand across 18 tasks and multiple logical layers.
findings.resolution: Split wp-web-cli into two packages: wp-web (covering tasks 5.1–5.10, locking src/assistant/web, priority 3) and wp-cli-serve (covering tasks 6.1–6.8, locking src/assistant/cli.py, priority 4, depending on wp-web). Update wp-integration to depend on wp-cli-serve.

findings.description: The JSON schemas for HarnessEvent and AGUIEvent describe the error field inconsistently with the redaction rule established in design decision D8.
findings.resolution: Update the description of the error property in openspec/changes/harness-ag-ui-bridge/contracts/events/harness-event.schema.json (RunFinished definition) and openspec/changes/harness-ag-ui-bridge/contracts/events/ag-ui-events.schema.json (RunFinished definition) to explicitly state: 'MUST be the exception class name only (e.g., RuntimeError) per the redaction rule in D8'.

findings.description: Spec scenarios lack stable IDs, which degrades test traceability as the project grows.
findings.resolution: Assign stable scenario IDs (e.g., #### Scenario [web-server.1]: ...) to all scenarios in the added spec deltas, following the project's emergent convention for structured testing.

findings.description: Rate limiting is correctly omitted for the v1 local-trust single-user scope.
Trusting sse-starlette for backpressure and disconnect detection is sufficient for the v1 local-bridge scope.
The warn-not-refuse posture for non-loopback binding is appropriate for v1 utility while maintaining visibility of the security posture.
